description: A7 distillation-fidelity corpus: verbose multi-claim sources, each annotated with the gold claims a faithful atomization must preserve. The source is distilled by SAM/IA's offline atomizer, the atoms are stored and recalled, and claim-preservation F1 is scored programmatically from key-term coverage (primary, no model). A separate paraphrase subset (judge_eval claims) is scored by a pinned local judge with saved transcripts (open-ended only).

sources.text: On the first of April 2023 Maria finally went down to the Springfield animal shelter and adopted a calico cat. She decided to name the cat Pixel. The shelter staff said Pixel had been waiting for a home for almost six months.

claims.probe: What kind of animal did Maria adopt?
What did Maria name her new cat?
Where did Maria adopt the cat from?
Did Maria take on responsibility for a new pet?
claims.paraphrase: Maria became the owner of a new pet companion.

sources.text: Over the long winter Lena spent her weekends in the garage. Piece by piece she assembled a reflecting telescope from a kit. By spring she could see the rings of Saturn through it.

claims.probe: What instrument did Lena build over the winter?
Where did Lena assemble the telescope?
What did Lena observe through her telescope?

sources.text: Diego spent the late summer carving a canoe out of cedar. When it was finished he carried it down to the lake and paddled all the way across to the far shore.

claims.probe: What wood did Diego use for his canoe?
Where did Diego paddle the canoe?

sources.text: Priya accepted an offer in the autumn of 2022. She had been interviewing for months. The role was a data engineering position, and it meant moving to Berlin. She started in September.

claims.probe: What job did Priya start in 2022?
Which city did Priya move to for the new job?
Did the new job require Priya to live somewhere else?
claims.paraphrase: Priya had to change the city she lived in for work.

sources.text: David had been saving for two years, and in 2021 he bought a brand-new sedan. The car was a blue Toyota. He drives it to work every morning across the bridge.

claims.probe: What make of car did David buy?
What colour was David's new car?
What body style was David's Toyota?

sources.text: Omar is proud of his small backyard greenhouse. In it he grows heirloom tomatoes, the kind you cannot buy in shops. He waters them every evening.

claims.probe: What does Omar grow in his greenhouse?
Where does Omar grow his tomatoes?

sources.text: Marcus trained all spring for the coastal marathon. On race day the weather was perfect. He crossed the finish line in just under four hours, a personal best.

claims.probe: What race did Marcus run in the spring?
Roughly how long did Marcus take to finish?
Was this Marcus's quickest time at this distance?
claims.paraphrase: It was the fastest Marcus had ever run that distance.

sources.text: Before the recital Sofia carefully restrung an old violin. It was not just any violin — it had belonged to her grandmother. She tuned each string by ear.

claims.probe: What did Sofia do to the violin before the recital?
Whose violin was it originally?
